description.p: gnome-clips provides a persistent clipboard history for GNOME desktop. Trigger it with Super+V to browse, search, and paste from your clipboard history.

Note: the Super+V hotkey is registered by a companion GNOME Shell extension (gnome-clips-toggle). Shell extensions cannot live inside a Flatpak sandbox; install the extension from your distribution ('apt install gnome-clips-extension' on Debian/Ubuntu) or from extensions.gnome.org.

ul.li: Stores text, images, files, HTML, and Markdown
Search, pin, and tag clipboard entries
Privacy controls: app exclusion list and incognito mode
